lessons.title: Time With Your Manager: About the Platform + the App
lessons.content_body:
Exercise1:30–2:00 PM

About the Platform + the App

Explore Groupon from a customer's point of view — first on your computer, then on your phone in the Groupon app, where about 70% of our sales happen. Browse your category and favorite a few deals you'd actually buy.

On the desktop site, consider:
  • What made you choose the deals you favorited?
  • How easy is the site to navigate?
  • Did any deal pages capture your attention — or turn you away?
  • What stood out, and what would you change?
Then in the app, consider:
  • What feels different from the desktop site?
  • How does browsing and buying change on a phone?
  • What stands out about how deals are presented?
  • How might this shape a customer's decision to buy?
Open video in new tab Slides Open Groupon.com

lessons.title: Time With Your Manager: Groupon Glossary
lessons.content_body:
Reference2:00–2:30 PM

Groupon Glossary

Review the Groupon Acronym Index (GAI) to get familiar with the terms you'll hear every day. Focus on the sales terms, then test yourself with the check below.

Open Glossary
Check for knowledge — sales terms
What does GP stand for, and why does it matter?
Gross Profit. Roughly NOR less cost of goods sold — it's the core measure of what a deal earns for Groupon.
What is VFM?
Vendor Funded Marketing. The merchant shares the cost of order-discount promotions on a per-unit basis (their share won't exceed 20% of the remittance amount).
What does NOR mean?
Net Operational Revenue. The revenue Groupon earns on a voucher after promo discount, same-day cancels, and credit card fees.
What's the difference between BD and MD?
BD = Business Development (the "hunters" who close new business). MD = Merchant Development (the "farmers" who keep merchants on the site, optimize running deals, and re-feature them).
What is the AM team responsible for?
Account Manager. Handles most merchant communication after a deal is closed — previews, copy edits, payment issues, and more.
What is DCT and what's it used for?
Deal Creation Tool. The unified platform for everyone involved in creating a deal — it replaces multiple legacy systems with a single workflow.
A merchant is on the DNR list. What does that mean for you?
Do Not Run. The merchant is too risky or has a history of poor service. Sales is not allowed to close these merchants to a deal.

lessons.title: Time With Your Manager: About the Platform + the App
lessons.content_body:
Exercise1:30–2:00 PM

About the Platform + the App

Explore Groupon from a customer's point of view — first on your computer, then on your phone in the Groupon app, where about 70% of our sales happen. Browse your category and favorite a few deals you'd actually buy.

On the desktop site, consider:
  • What made you choose the deals you favorited?
  • How easy is the site to navigate?
  • Did any deal pages capture your attention — or turn you away?
  • What stood out, and what would you change?
Then in the app, consider:
  • What feels different from the desktop site?
  • How does browsing and buying change on a phone?
  • What stands out about how deals are presented?
  • How might this shape a customer's decision to buy?
Open video in new tab Slides Open Groupon.com

lessons.title: Time With Your Manager: Groupon Glossary
lessons.content_body:
Reference2:00–2:30 PM

Groupon Glossary

Review the Groupon Acronym Index (GAI) to get familiar with the terms you'll hear every day. Focus on the sales terms, then test yourself with the check below.

Open Glossary
Check for knowledge — sales terms
What does GP stand for, and why does it matter?
Gross Profit. Roughly NOR less cost of goods sold — it's the core measure of what a deal earns for Groupon.
What is VFM?
Vendor Funded Marketing. The merchant shares the cost of order-discount promotions on a per-unit basis (their share won't exceed 20% of the remittance amount).
What does NOR mean?
Net Operational Revenue. The revenue Groupon earns on a voucher after promo discount, same-day cancels, and credit card fees.
What's the difference between BD and MD?
BD = Business Development (the "hunters" who close new business). MD = Merchant Development (the "farmers" who keep merchants on the site, optimize running deals, and re-feature them).
What is the AM team responsible for?
Account Manager. Handles most merchant communication after a deal is closed — previews, copy edits, payment issues, and more.
What is DCT and what's it used for?
Deal Creation Tool. The unified platform for everyone involved in creating a deal — it replaces multiple legacy systems with a single workflow.
A merchant is on the DNR list. What does that mean for you?
Do Not Run. The merchant is too risky or has a history of poor service. Sales is not allowed to close these merchants to a deal.

lessons.title: Time With Your Manager: About the Platform + the App
lessons.content_body:
Exercise1:30–2:00 PM

About the Platform + the App

Explore Groupon from a customer's point of view — first on your computer, then on your phone in the Groupon app, where about 70% of our sales happen. Browse your category and favorite a few deals you'd actually buy.

On the desktop site, consider:
  • What made you choose the deals you favorited?
  • How easy is the site to navigate?
  • Did any deal pages capture your attention — or turn you away?
  • What stood out, and what would you change?
Then in the app, consider:
  • What feels different from the desktop site?
  • How does browsing and buying change on a phone?
  • What stands out about how deals are presented?
  • How might this shape a customer's decision to buy?
Open video in new tab Slides Open Groupon.com

lessons.title: Time With Your Manager: Groupon Glossary
lessons.content_body:
Reference2:00–2:30 PM

Groupon Glossary

Review the Groupon Acronym Index (GAI) to get familiar with the terms you'll hear every day. Focus on the sales terms, then test yourself with the check below.

Open Glossary
Check for knowledge — sales terms
What does GP stand for, and why does it matter?
Gross Profit. Roughly NOR less cost of goods sold — it's the core measure of what a deal earns for Groupon.
What is VFM?
Vendor Funded Marketing. The merchant shares the cost of order-discount promotions on a per-unit basis (their share won't exceed 20% of the remittance amount).
What does NOR mean?
Net Operational Revenue. The revenue Groupon earns on a voucher after promo discount, same-day cancels, and credit card fees.
What's the difference between BD and MD?
BD = Business Development (the "hunters" who close new business). MD = Merchant Development (the "farmers" who keep merchants on the site, optimize running deals, and re-feature them).
What is the AM team responsible for?
Account Manager. Handles most merchant communication after a deal is closed — previews, copy edits, payment issues, and more.
What is DCT and what's it used for?
Deal Creation Tool. The unified platform for everyone involved in creating a deal — it replaces multiple legacy systems with a single workflow.
A merchant is on the DNR list. What does that mean for you?
Do Not Run. The merchant is too risky or has a history of poor service. Sales is not allowed to close these merchants to a deal.

lessons.title: Time With Your Manager: About the Platform + the App
lessons.content_body:
Exercise1:30–2:00 PM

About the Platform + the App

Explore Groupon from a customer's point of view — first on your computer, then on your phone in the Groupon app, where about 70% of our sales happen. Browse your category and favorite a few deals you'd actually buy.

On the desktop site, consider:
  • What made you choose the deals you favorited?
  • How easy is the site to navigate?
  • Did any deal pages capture your attention — or turn you away?
  • What stood out, and what would you change?
Then in the app, consider:
  • What feels different from the desktop site?
  • How does browsing and buying change on a phone?
  • What stands out about how deals are presented?
  • How might this shape a customer's decision to buy?
Open video in new tab Slides Open Groupon.com

lessons.title: Time With Your Manager: Groupon Glossary
lessons.content_body:
Reference2:00–2:30 PM

Groupon Glossary

Review the Groupon Acronym Index (GAI) to get familiar with the terms you'll hear every day. Focus on the sales terms, then test yourself with the check below.

Open Glossary
Check for knowledge — sales terms
What does GP stand for, and why does it matter?
Gross Profit. Roughly NOR less cost of goods sold — it's the core measure of what a deal earns for Groupon.
What is VFM?
Vendor Funded Marketing. The merchant shares the cost of order-discount promotions on a per-unit basis (their share won't exceed 20% of the remittance amount).
What does NOR mean?
Net Operational Revenue. The revenue Groupon earns on a voucher after promo discount, same-day cancels, and credit card fees.
What's the difference between BD and MD?
BD = Business Development (the "hunters" who close new business). MD = Merchant Development (the "farmers" who keep merchants on the site, optimize running deals, and re-feature them).
What is the AM team responsible for?
Account Manager. Handles most merchant communication after a deal is closed — previews, copy edits, payment issues, and more.
What is DCT and what's it used for?
Deal Creation Tool. The unified platform for everyone involved in creating a deal — it replaces multiple legacy systems with a single workflow.
A merchant is on the DNR list. What does that mean for you?
Do Not Run. The merchant is too risky or has a history of poor service. Sales is not allowed to close these merchants to a deal.
